title: New Deep-Sea Creatures Found in the Ocean
summary: Scientists have found 24 new types of tiny animals called amphipods in the deep sea of the Pacific Ocean. This area is called the Clarion-Clipperton Zone, and it is very big and not well understood. The scientists worked together in a special workshop to study these animals. They found one group that is so different it is called a new superfamily, which is very rare. This discovery helps us learn more about life in the deep sea, where many species are still unknown. The project is part of a bigger effort to name 1,000 new species by 2030. By identifying these animals, scientists can protect the ocean better and share knowledge about its amazing creatures.

background_read: The deep sea is a dark, cold part of the ocean where many strange animals live. Scientists explore it to discover new species and understand how life survives there. The Clarion-Clipperton Zone is a huge area in the Pacific Ocean that is hard to study because it is so deep. Amphipods are tiny creatures that can be predators or scavengers, eating other animals or dead stuff. Finding new species helps scientists protect the ocean and learn about biodiversity, which means all the different types of life on Earth.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists led by Dr. Anna Jażdżewska and Tammy Horton
WHAT: Found 24 new amphipod species including a new superfamily
WHERE: In the Clarion-Clipperton Zone in the Pacific Ocean
WHY: To learn about deep-sea life and help protect the ocean

perspectives.description: Finding new species helps us understand biodiversity in the deep sea.
